key_insight: A D-gun applied Al2O3-TiO2 coating substantially improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest best predicts wear from test conditions.
engineering_relevance: This supports using detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings and data-driven wear models to extend the service life of aerospace, biomedical, and marine titanium components.
method: Applied an ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating to Ti-6Al-4V using D-gun processing under controlled oxygen/acetylene flow and stand-off distance.
Evaluated tribological performance with ASTM G99 pin-on-disc wear tests against EN31 steel at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM showed reduced wear and protective tribo-layer formation on coated surfaces, and random forest had the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the tested models.
watch_item: The conclusions are based on the reported test window and counterface conditions, so model transferability to other loads, speeds, environments, or geometries should be validated before deployment.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
